data.body:
OPTION 2 INVESTIGATION (why a session wrote the pointer with session=None):

MECHANISM (confirmed, not a code defect): every pointer write goes through start() -> _write_pointer(id, _resolve_session_fp()) (session_log.py:139, reached by append() on first-use/rotation at :169). So the pointer's session field == _resolve_session_fp() at write time. _resolve_session_fp() (session_log.py:47-63) returns CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID -> REBAR_SESSION_ID -> SESSION_ID -> None, DELIBERATELY stopping at None (no git-HEAD fallback, to avoid rotating mid-session on each commit). Therefore a session=None pointer is written iff the writing process had ALL THREE env vars unset. The write path is correct; this is environment-or-config, not a bug.

SCOPE: grep of .github/ + scripts/ shows NO committed automation calls session-log/append_session_log/log_session — so CI/cron/hooks are NOT fingerprint-less writers. The fingerprint-less writer of the observed pointer (log 95f1) was therefore an interactive/library/MCP/manual context lacking the env vars (e.g. a Claude surface/version not exporting CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID, or a subprocess not inheriting it, or a pre-harness-var session). Cannot pinpoint the specific past process (no env capture).

WHY NO AUTOMATIC IN-REPO FIX: there is no SAFE fallback rebar can invent for a no-id context — git HEAD rotates per commit, PID/CLI-invocation rotates per call. That is exactly why _resolve_session_fp stops at None and the design is degrade-safe. Contrast transition_close._resolve_session (:339-345) which DOES fall back to git HEAD/'unknown' for PROVENANCE (never None) — acceptable there because it is not used for rotation.

IMPACT NOW: option 1 (landed, Gerrit 323) already eliminated the harmful symptom — a FINGERPRINTED session now rotates off a session=None pointer instead of polluting it. The only residual is two DISTINCT no-id sessions sharing one log (both now=None -> no rotation), which is inherent and low-impact.

RECOMMENDED DISPOSITION (env/docs, no code defect): (a) rely on option 1 for the common case; (b) for any non-Claude context that legitimately writes session logs, set REBAR_SESSION_ID to a stable per-session value; (c) optionally document REBAR_SESSION_ID as the session-log rotation fallback in docs/session-log guidance (currently documented only for provenance in docs/config.md:332). No further code change proposed.
